items.note: The time-machine premise progresses and later callbacks assume prior use.

citations.text: is making adjustments to his time machine.
is is disappointed his time machine is still not working. I'll try again yesterday.
has just adjusted the flurb and installed the new flux capacitor. Time machine testing tomorrow, I'll call you yesterday if all goes well.
flew his time machine to the west coast and was able to successfully travel 2 hours into the future, that's progress.

wants to do away with the phrase "In today's economy". Everything you do is in "today's economy" unless you have a time machine...... If you do have a time machine (that works), call me.....I have some ideas.
I just found out I have jury duty in Tyler last week. I hope my time machine works better than it did the last time I used it.
can't find my flux capacitor.

items.note: A distinctive workplace-reporting frame recurs with new content.

citations.text: Overheard at work: "She was a communist, but I didn't realize that when I married her because I was in love with her at the time".
Overheard at work - Computer Geek insult attempt: You're so dumb you probably tried to buy a kindle and an iPad on the dame day.....

items.note: A mock-altruistic closing recurs in unrelated situations.

citations.text: Sometimes people go to a public restroom and leave a drink for the next visitor. Paying it forward people.
The only coffee in the hotel room this morning was decaffeinated. I made it just so I could throw it away. Pay it forward people.

items.note: A named persona is introduced and reused more than a year later.

citations.text: I’m going to be a GrandBrad.
Look who came to see GrandBrad!

items.note: Annual posts explicitly develop the same failure narrative.

citations.text: Whelp, missed National Daughters Day again. I think this is the only photo I have of her. Sorry Megan Cooke
Megan Cooke I thought national daughters day meant my daughter was supposed to post about me. I’m sorry I messed this up for the 20th consecutive time.
Happy day after National Daughters day Megan Cooke. #fatheroftheyear

items.note: Text introduces and revisits Fred, but image context is missing.

citations.text: Introducing Fred.
Fred offers his opinion on a passing car.

items.note: The named event recurs but both posts come from one weekend.

citations.text: DON'T FORGET ABOUT SUZYPALOOZA!!
suzypalooza 2015 complete. Preparations for suzypalooza 2016 start tomorrow.

items.note: A distinctive circus premise remains standalone.

citations.text: I've decided to join the circus....Where do I do that?
"I know we're going to be sent to the prison asteroid, and we deserve it."

items.note: An invented blended website remains standalone.

citations.text: Apparently I've logged into Chick-Fil-a-Obama-Romney-book.com

items.note: The Zark Muckerberg parody remains standalone.

citations.text: THANK YOU, ZARK MUCKERBERG, for your backward-thinking malevolence! And congrats on becoming a newt! Zark Muckerberg has announced that he is giving away $45 billion of chicken socks. What you may not have heard is that he plans to give .01% of it away to bed-wetters like YOU and YOUR MOM! All you have to do is copy and paste this message onto a fence post EVENTUALLY. At mildnight PST, Krampus will search through his underwear drawer and award 1000 mouth breathers with 4.5 socks EACH as a way of saying screw you for making meth such a powerful vehicle for contention. By the way this was on the newts!
Giant flying lobsters of Mexico.
